Craft a unique deck, encounter bizarre creatures, and discover relics of immense power in Slay the Spire 2! Recent Reviews: Mostly Positive (85,557) - 79% of the 85,557 user reviews in the last 30 days are positive. English Reviews: Overwhelmingly Positive (40,544) - 95% of the 40,544 user reviews for this game are positive. Release Date: 5 Mar, 2026 Developer: Mega Crit Publisher: Mega Crit Tags Popular user-defined tags for this product: Strategy Roguelike Card Game Deckbuilding Co-op Roguelike Deckbuilder Multiplayer Turn-Based Combat Card Battler Indie Dungeon Crawler Roguelite 2D Early Access Difficult Singleplayer Fantasy Replay Value Procedural Generation Mouse only + Reviews All Reviews: English Reviews Overwhelmingly Positive (95% of 40,544) Recent Reviews: Recent: Mostly Positive (79% of 85,557) Sign in to add this item to your wishlist, follow it, or mark it as ignored Links & info Features Single-player Online Co-op Steam Cloud Stats Steam Leaderboards Family Sharing Languages : English and 13 more Interface Full Audio Subtitles English ✔ French ✔ Italian ✔ German ✔ Spanish - Spain ✔ Japanese ✔ Korean ✔ Polish ✔ Portuguese - Brazil ✔ Russian ✔ Simplified Chinese ✔ Spanish - Latin America ✔ Thai ✔ Turkish ✔ See all 14 supported languages Content Includes Interactive Elements Online interactivity Title: Slay the Spire 2 Genre: Indie , Strategy , Early Access Developer: Mega Crit Publisher: Mega Crit Release Date: 5 Mar, 2026 Early Access Release Date: 5 Mar, 2026 Visit the website Discord Reddit X Bluesky TikTok View update history Read related news View discussions Find Community Groups Embed Early Access Game Get instant access and start playing; get involved with this game as it develops. Note: Games in Early Access are not complete and may or may not change further. If you are not excited to play this game in its current state, then you should wait to see if the game progresses further in development. Learn more What the developers have to say: Why Early Access? “Slay the Spire requires a lot of player feedback so we can balance content, add quality of life features, and make sure the game runs without issues. Early Access is also a chance for us to test experimental features, try exotic designs, identify niche problems, and helps us make sure the game is headed in the right direction.” Approximately how long will this game be in Early Access? “Until the game feels great! Last time we thought it would be about a year and it took about 1.5 years. So perhaps in the same 1 - 2 year range.” How is the full version planned to differ from the Early Access version? “We plan to add more gameplay content, a true ending, various game modes, improved balance, a ton of bug fixes, greater hardware compatibility, fancier visual effects, and the game will be available in more languages.” What is the current state of the Early Access version? “Please refer to the ABOUT THIS GAME section for details.” Will the game be priced differently during and after Early Access? “The price of the game will increase after Early Access.” How are you planning on involving the Community in your development process? “We'll primarily be listening to feedback in our Discord server but will also be monitoring other social media to see what's going well and what's going poorly. The game will also include the gathering of anonymized metrics and an in-game feedback tool to help players provide feedback.” Read more Buy Slay the Spire 2 ¥ 2,800 Add to Cart Buy RACCOIN + Slay the Spire 2 BUNDLE (?) Includes 2 items: RACCOIN: Coin Pusher Roguelike , Slay the Spire 2 Bundle info -5% -6% ¥ 3,895 ¥ 3,673 Add to Cart Add to Account Buy Slay the Spire Collection BUNDLE (?) Includes 2 items: Slay the Spire , Slay the Spire 2 Bundle info -10% ¥ 5,040 Add to Cart Add to Account View Community Hub See all discussions Report bugs and leave feedback for this game on the discussion boards About This Game THE SPIRE AWAKENS The ultimate roguelike deckbuilder returns! For 1,000 years, the Spire lay dormant, its secrets buried and its horrors forgotten. Now, it has reopened, hungrier and more dangerous than ever, devouring all who dare to ascend. New perils demand sharper strategies, relentless cunning, and unwavering resolve. Outwit the Spire’s brutal trials and uncover the truths hidden at its peak. Will you attempt the climb alone or enlist the aid of your fellow adventurers? AN EVER-CHANGING SPIRE Step into the newly evolved Spire, a twisted labyrinth teeming with strange and deadly foes. Adapt to an arsenal of brand new cards, relics, and potions, each offering game-changing possibilities—or dangerous consequences. Climb the Spire with a new and returning cast of characters, each with their own cards, motives, and secrets. No two climbs are ever the same. Unpredictable challenges and evolving strategies await those brave—or foolish—enough to ascend. CLIMB ALONE OR WITH ALLIES Brave the ascent on your own, or play with up to 4 players and face the Spire together in the all new co-op mode. Discover multiplayer-specific cards, powerful team synergies, and carry your friends (or get carried) to victory! A NEW AND RETURNING CAST OF CHARACTERS Embark on your quest to Slay the Spire as one of five different characters, some of whom will be familiar to seasoned Slayers and two of whom who are brand new. Discover each character’s unique playstyle, full potential, and secret backstory as you conquer all who stand in their way. UNCOVER THE SPIRE’S LORE The history of the Spire and its inhabitants, be they friend or foe, can now be discovered in entirely new ways. Unlock fragments of its mysterious timeline and encounter the most ancient of its residents through your myriad attempts. MORE TO COME IN EARLY ACCESS The Spire truly is ever-changing… in more ways than one. More content—including cards, events, environments, enemies, and more—will be added and balanced throughout Early Access. Join the journey early to be a part of the Spire’s next evolution. Why release in Early Access, you ask? Slay the Spire's success comes from our community! It sounds corny, but the extra mile many of you went to report issues, translate content/announcements, create long video essays, make excellent (lol) tier lists, and draw goofy or gorgeous fan art is the reason we're doing it all again. We love our job! System Requirements Windows macOS SteamOS + Linux Minimum: Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system OS: Windows 10 (64-bit) Processor: Dual-core 2.0 GHz Memory: 4 GB RAM Graphics: DirectX 12 or Vulkan compatible, 1 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space Recommended: Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system OS: Windows 10 (64-bit) Processor: Quad-core 3.0 GHz Memory: 8 GB RAM Graphics: DirectX 12 or Vulkan compatible, 2 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space Minimum: OS: macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) Processor: Intel Core i5 (dual-core) Memory: 4 GB RAM Graphics: Metal-compatible GPU, 1 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space Recommended: OS: macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) Processor: Apple Silicon M1 / Intel Core i5 (quad-core) Memory: 8 GB RAM Graphics: Metal-compatible GPU, 2 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space Minimum: OS: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS / SteamOS 3.0 Processor: Dual-core CPU, 2.0 GHz (x86_64) Memory: 4 GB RAM Graphics: Vulkan 1.0 or OpenGL 3.3 compatible, 1 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space Recommended: OS: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS / SteamOS 3.0 Processor: Quad-core CPU, 3.0 GHz (x86_64) Memory: 8 GB RAM Graphics: Vulkan 1.0 or OpenGL 3.3 compatible, 1 GB VRAM Storage: 4 GB available space View all What Curators Say 208 Curators have reviewed this product. 此游戏的 43,139 篇用户评测中有 63% 为好评。 发行日期: 2026 年 3 月 5 日 开发者: Mega Crit 发行商: Mega Crit 标签 该产品的热门用户自定义标签： 策略 类 Rogue 卡牌游戏 牌组构建 合作 牌组构建式类 Rogue 多人 回合制战斗 卡牌战斗 独立 迷宫探索 轻度 Rogue 2D 抢先体验 困难 单人 奇幻 重玩价值 程序生成 仅鼠标 + 评测 所有评测： 简体中文评测 褒贬不一 (43,139 篇中的 63%) 最近评测： 最近： 多半好评 (85,557 篇中的 79%) 想要将此项目添加至您的愿望单、关注它或标记为已忽略，请先 登录 链接与信息 功能 单人 在线合作 Steam 云 统计数据 Steam 排行榜 家庭共享 语言 : 简体中文及其他 13 种语言 界面 完全音频 字幕 简体中文 ✔ 英语 ✔ 法语 ✔ 意大利语 ✔ 德语 ✔ 西班牙语 - 西班牙 ✔ 日语 ✔ 韩语 ✔ 波兰语 ✔ 葡萄牙语 - 巴西 ✔ 俄语 ✔ 西班牙语 - 拉丁美洲 ✔ 泰语 ✔ 土耳其语 ✔ 查看所有 14 种已支持语言 内容 包括互动元素 在线互动 名称: Slay the Spire 2 类型: 独立 , 策略 , 抢先体验 开发者: Mega Crit 发行商: Mega Crit 发行日期: 2026 年 3 月 5 日 抢先体验发行日期: 2026 年 3 月 5 日 访问网站 Discord Reddit X Bluesky TikTok 查看更新记录 阅读相关新闻 查看讨论 查找社区组 嵌入 抢先体验游戏 立刻获取体验权限然后开始游戏，并随着游戏的发展参与其中。 注意： 处于抢先体验的游戏内容尚不完整且也许会发生变化。如果您不是特别想玩当前这个状态下的游戏，那么您应该等待一段时间来观察游戏的开发进度。 了解更多 开发者的话： 为何要采用抢先体验这种模式？ “《杀戮尖塔》的开发就依赖了许多玩家们的反馈来帮助平衡内容和增加易用性功能，当然还有确保游戏能够顺畅运行。抢先体验让我们可以尝试一些实验性的功能、比较独特的设计、发现小众问题、帮助我们确认游戏正在正确的道路上前进。” 这款游戏的抢先体验状态大约持续多久？ “直到游戏感觉够棒！1代时，我们以为抢先体验会持续大约1年，但最终花了1年半，所以或许这次也会在1-2年的范围之内吧。” 计划中的完整版本和抢先体验版本到底有多少不同？ “我们计划加入更多游玩内容、真结局、不同的游戏模式、改善平衡性、修复各种Bug、提高硬件兼容性、加入更华丽的特效、以及让游戏提供更多的语言版本。” 抢先体验版本的现状如何？ “请查阅下方 “关于此游戏” 的部分来获取详情。” 在抢先体验期间和结束之后，游戏价格会有所不同吗？ “游戏价格将会在抢先体验结束后上调。” 在开发过程中，你们是如何计划让玩家社区参与进来的？ “我们会首先听取我们Discord服务器中的意见，但同时也会时刻关注其他社交媒体，了解游戏受欢迎与不受欢迎的地方。游戏同时会收集匿名的统计数据，也有游戏内的反馈工具，协助玩家们提交各种反馈。” 了解更多 购买 Slay the Spire 2 ¥ 2,800 添加至购物车 购买 RACCOIN + Slay the Spire 2 捆绑包 (?) 包含 2 件物品： 浣熊推币机 , Slay the Spire 2 捆绑包信息 -5% -6% ¥ 3,895 ¥ 3,673 添加至购物车 添加到帐户 购买 Slay the Spire Collection 捆绑包 (?) 包含 2 件物品： Slay the Spire , Slay the Spire 2 捆绑包信息 -10% ¥ 5,040 添加至购物车 添加到帐户 浏览社区中心 查看所有讨论 在讨论版中为这款游戏汇报 BUG，并留下反馈 关于此游戏 高塔苏醒了 究极的卡牌肉鸽游戏再度归来！ 一千年来，高塔一直在沉睡。其中的秘密被深埋、恐怖被遗忘。如今，高塔再次开启，比以往更加饥饿与危险，吞噬每一个胆敢前来攀升的人。 新的危险要求你使用更敏锐的策略、最无情的计算与毫不动摇的决心。克服高塔残酷的考验，发现藏在它顶峰的真相吧。 你是会独自攀爬，还是征求其他冒险者同伴们的支持呢？ 不断变化的高塔 踏进已经焕然一新的高塔，这里是一个充满奇异与致命敌人的扭曲迷宫。试着熟悉全新的卡牌、遗物与药水，每一样都有可能改变你的整局游戏——或是带来危险的后果。 和众多新老角色一起爬塔，每位角色都有自己的卡牌、动机和秘密。 没有哪两次攀登会一模一样。不可预料的挑战与不断进化的策略等待着那些勇敢——或愚昧——的爬塔者们。 独自攀登，或与盟友共同进退 是独自挑战高塔，还是最多4名玩家一起在全新的合作模式中共同面对高塔，决定在你！发现多人模式专属的卡牌、强大的团队连携、扛起你的队友们（或是被队友们扛着）去获得胜利！ 众多新老角色 从五名不同的角色中选择一位踏上爬塔的旅程，有一些角色对老玩家们会相当熟悉，而另两位则是全新登场。在扫清一切拦路敌人的途中，发现每一位角色的独特游玩风格、发挥他们的全部潜力、了解他们秘密的背景故事。 发现高塔的故事 高塔的历史和其中众多无论敌友的居民，如今你都可以用全新的方式发现和了解他们。解锁高塔神秘时间线中的无数碎片，在你无数次的爬塔旅程中，邂逅其中最为古老的居民们。 抢先体验期间还会有更多内容 高塔总是不断变化……并且变化的层面多种多样。更多内容——包括卡牌、事件、环境、敌人和更多——都会在抢先体验期间不断添加和调整平衡。来抢先加入这段旅程，成为高塔下一次进化的一部分吧。 那么，为什么要用抢先体验的模式发布呢？你可能会问。因为《杀戮尖塔1》的成功就源于我们的社区！这听起来很俗套，但当初众多玩家们为游戏所付出的额外助力，无论是汇报问题、翻译内容与新闻、发布长篇论文式视频、制作优秀的（哈哈）强度表/排行榜、还是绘制各种精美或搞怪的同人图，这一切都是让我们能够再次制作续作的原因。我们热爱我们的工作！ 系统需求 Windows macOS SteamOS + Linux title: Bosses
text: Bosses are extremely difficult monsters that are fought at the end of each act. Compared to normal enemies and elites, bosses have significantly more health and a more powerful move set that usually involves special gimmicks that cripple the player or heavily buffs itself. Every boss has a unique portrait shown at the top of the Spire map. The map location prior to a boss fight will always be a Rest Site, allowing the player to heal before the fight. The only exception to this is the in Act 4 which always has an elite encounter containing the and . Ascension Modifiers - : Bosses deal more damage. - : Bosses have more health and will sometimes gain more . - : Bosses have more challenging movesets and abilities. - : Two bosses are fought in a row at the end of Act 3. Rewards Upon defeating the Act 1 and Act 2 bosses, the player is rewarded with Gold, a choice between three , a choice between three , and sometimes a random Potion. The player is healed for 100% () of their missing health at the end of every boss battle. The Act 3 and Act 4 bosses do not reward any consumables or cards. Killing a boss will add to a run's Score. Killing it without taking any damage, not including self-damaging cards like , adds another 50 points. Killing all three bosses without taking any damage adds 200 points. Interactions Relics - : Heals for 25 HP at the start of a boss encounter. - : Gain an extra Energy at the start of each Boss and Elite encounter. cannot be used to skip boss fights, as all paths in the map will lead to the boss. Other During the event, choosing starts a fight with a random Act 1 boss for a random . This fight does not add towards the run's Score, but will activate the above relics as normal. This fight will not count towards the achievement . To fight the , the player must kill the Act 3 boss with the Ironclad, Silent, and Defect at least once. This means the Heart cannot be fought on the first playthrough of any character except for the Watcher. Act 1 Bosses One of these bosses will appear on floor 16. 300px|class=boss-img|link=Slime Boss Slime Boss 300px|class=boss-img|link=The Guardian The Guardian 300px|class=boss-img|link=Hexaghost Hexaghost Act 2 Bosses One of these bosses will appear on floor 33. 300px|class=boss-img|link=Bronze Automaton Bronze Automaton 300px|class=boss-img|link=The Collector The Collector 300px|class=boss-img|link=The Champ The Champ Act 3 Bosses One of these bosses will appear on floor 50. On , a second boss (different from the first) will appear on floor 51. 300px|class=boss-img|link=Awakened One Awakened One 300px|class=boss-img|link=Time Eater Time Eater 300px|class=boss-img|link=Donu and Deca Donu and Deca Act 4 Boss The Heart appears on floor 55 (). 400px|class=boss-img|link=Corrupt Heart Corrupt Heart Category:Bosses

title: Cards
text: At the start of a run, all 4 characters have access to a starting deck of 10 or 12 cards. Each character has their own version of Strike and Defend, all of which function identically, save their appearance. In addition to these Strikes and Defends, each character has 1-2 starting cards that help enable and distinguish their starting deck's composition. Additional cards are obtained via Monsters, Events, Shops, and certain Relics. Card Types All cards in the game can be sorted into one of these five types. - are cards that deal direct damage and sometimes have a secondary effect. These cards have a rectangular border with the bottom edge being tapered down. - are cards that have diverse effects usually through providing , utilities, buffs, or debuffs. They are distinct from Attack cards due to never dealing damage directly, however they are capable of doing so indirectly via debuffs (such as ) or utilities (such as Defect's orbs). These cards have a rectangular border. - are cards that give a buff that lasts throughout the combat encounter. Each copy of a power can only be played once per combat. Some powers give flat stat bonuses (such as or ), require certain conditions to be met during combat, or even provide unique buffs that can drastically alter playstyle. These cards have an oval border. - are cards designed to bloat your deck and are unable to be played with the exception of . They are primarily meant to stop you from drawing beneficial cards and some of them have additional effects. Statuses are obtained during combat and are removed from the deck at the end of combat. - are unplayable cards added to the deck during in-game events. They are similar to Status cards but they persist in the deck until removed by other means and typically have more severe effects. is the only curse that can be played. Some Curses cannot be removed from your deck at all. These include , , and . Colorless - cards can be obtained by any class through the Shop, Events, or from normal card rewards via . Colorless cards have diverse effects, and some of them can only be obtained via unique sources such as . (These are marked as Special rarity) Card Rarity The rarity of the card is indicated in the color of the banner behind the name. - cards have a grey banner and frame. - cards have a blue banner and frame. - cards have a golden banner and frame. - cards are the default cards from the starting deck for your character. They have the same grey banner and frame as Commons, though certain events treat them as a lower tier when offered or transformed. - cards are only obtained from a limited amount of sources, which is usually just one. Special cards are always Colorless, meaning that all classes can obtain them given they have the means to do so. Card Distribution The chance to get a Rare card starts off at the below percentages minus 5%. The chance increases by 1% with every Common card rolled, sequentially. This offset is subtracted from the chance for Common cards (then Uncommons, if necessary). When a Rare card is rolled, the chance resets back to the listed value, minus 5%. As a result, your first floor in Act 1, Act 2 and Act 3 can never have a Rare card, except in special circumstances and Neow's Boss Swap, an advantage as part of Neow's Third Blessing and the Choose a Card Neow choice can make it possible to get a Rare card on Floor 1. Clicking both those boss relics as your Act 1 or 2 boss reward will make it possible for your next floor to drop a Rare card. and can never give a Rare card as it comes directly after obtaining a rare card. Base chance pre-offset in normal encounters Base chance pre-offset in Elite encounters Base chance pre-offset in Shops Only cards from card rewards (including and ) affect the offset. Shop Cards and cards from the do not affect rare offset, however they are affected by rare chance. works like 5 sequential card rewards, except it uses the base chance in Shops. That is, it both affects and is affected by the offset. Effects that generate cards mid-combat are unaffected by Card Rarity, except 15% Rare, 30% Uncommon, 55% Common Card Generation Cards can be temporarily added to a deck via Cards (Such as or ), Potions (Such as or ), Relics (Such as or ), or enemy attacks (one exception being , which remains permanently.). - Generated cards are unaffected by Card Rarity, except (weighed as 15% Rare, 30% Uncommon, 55% Common). - They can never be generated upgraded, unless is active. - Master Reality does not stack for upgrades on generation. - If generated mid-combat by anything except and , will be upgraded twice, rather than just once. This includes when have died after taking a Searing Blow, which is the only way to get this interaction without the use of mods, , or Custom Modifiers. - Cards with a Healing tag are exempt from generating. These cards are , , , , , , and . Notably, can be generated. Beta Art Beta Art is a feature that allows you to replace the normal card art with the early development version of the card art. These often include various puns or references to other popular media. Beta Art for a character's cards is unlocked when beating the . Beta art for cards and are unlocked after beating the with all characters. Beta art is toggled for each card individually. - For PC: Right click to inspect a card at any point and then toggle on the Beta Art option. Inspecting a card through the Compendium also shows this toggle. - For Console/Mobile: Inspect a card in the Compendium and toggle on Beta Art. Notes - Cards shown as X-cost can still be played even without any Energy, although this makes their effects do nothing unless X is increased by some amount. For example, an Upgraded will one even if played without any Energy. This can be used to advance the effects of relics such as and without spending any Energy. Category:Game Mechanics

title: Elites
text: Elites are highly powerful enemies that are found in Elite Encounters which are marked separately from normal monster encounters. Defeating an elite will give you a random Relic, 25-35 Gold, a Card reward, and a Score bonus. The same elite can appear multiple times in an act, but cannot be encountered twice in a row. The event ignores this. Ascension Modifiers - : Elites are around 60% more common on the map. - : Elites do more damage. - : Elites have more health as well as sometimes gain more . - : Elites have more challenging movesets and abilities. Super Elites After unlocking Act 4, until the Emerald Key is obtained, one of the elites in each Act will appear as a burning elite icon. This elite will receive one of the following buffs and drop the Emerald Key when defeated. - [Act Number + 1] - +25% Max HP - [Act Number * 2 + 2] - [Act Number * 2 + 1] Relic Interactions - : Elites will drop an extra relic - : Start each Elite combat with 2 - : All enemies in elite rooms will start combat with 25% less HP (and become visually smaller) - : During Boss or Elite encounters, you gain an extra Energy at the start of your turn With the exception of , these relics also work in the fight and the second fight of the . Act 1 Elites class=elite-img|link=Gremlin Nob Gremlin Nob class=elite-img|link=Lagavulin Lagavulin class=elite-img|link=Sentry Sentries Act 2 Elites class=elite-img|link=Book of Stabbing Book of Stabbing class=elite-img|link=Gremlin Leader Gremlin Leader class=elite-img|link=Taskmaster Taskmaster Act 3 Elites class=elite-img|link=Giant Head Giant Head class=elite-img|link=Nemesis Nemesis 200x200px|class=elite-img|link=Reptomancer Reptomancer Act 4 Elites class=elite-img|link=Spire Shield and Spire Spear Spire Shield and Spire Spear Category:Elites

title: Events
text: Category:ToUpdate Events are unique encounters that present the player with a scenario and asks them to make a choice on how they wish to handle that scenario. These scenarios can involve running into various denizens of the Spire (both helpful and harmful), triggering traps, finding shrines and altars, wandering into something strange, and much more. Events are only encountered in Unknown floors. Each Act has a set of events that can only occur in that Act, but there are also events which can occur in two or three Acts. No events can occur in Act 4 as there are no unknown floors to travel to. Starting from , some events have less positive outcomes and/or more severe consequences. For example: more HP lost, less gold rewarded, higher prices, or a higher likelihood of being . Encountering Events Events are not the only type of room that can be encountered in unknown floors. Whenever the player enters an unknown floor, there is a high chance that they will encounter an Event and a set of much lower chances that they encounter a Monster, Shop, or Treasure Room. Each room type has a different chance of being encountered, which the game determines randomly when entering the unknown floor. Many events have a set of criteria that must be met in order for them to be encountered. All of an event's criteria must be met for it to be eligible. Some example criteria are: - Being in a specific Act (e.g. is exclusive to Act 1) - Being under or above a specific floor (e.g. can only be encountered within Act 1 on floor 7 or above) - Having enough Gold to afford at least one of the event's options (e.g. requires at least 35 Gold) - Having a specific amount of HP remaining (e.g. requires at least 13 HP, while requires HP to be at or below 50%) - ... and many more. Some events have very unique and specific criteria (e.g. requires at least 2 Relics, and requires a ) If the game determines that an unknown floor should be an event, there is a high chance that it will be an Act Event and a lower chance that it will be one of the much rarer Shrine Events. If the event should be one of the rarer Shrine Events, the game will combine the eligible Shrine and One-Time Event pools and then pick a random event from that combined pool. Types of Events While there is no in-game distinction between event types, it can be useful to understand the internal details because different event types have different rules on how they can be encountered. Internally, there are 3 types of events: Act Events, Shrines, and One-Time Events. Note: There is an "unofficial" event type which the Slay the Spire community calls "Shrine Events". This is different from (but related to) Shrines. The language used on this page is very careful to correctly refer to "Shrines" or "Shrine Events" depending on the context, as these are two separate terms that do not mean the same thing but are closely related and easy to mix up. Read those sections for more information. See the table below for a quick summary of the differences between event types. Act Events Internally, these are simply called Events . Act events can be considered as the baseline "normal events". They can all only be encountered in a specific Act, and can each only be encountered once. For example, is an event that is exclusive to Act 1. Once the player encounters it in Act 1 they will not see that event again for the remainder of Act 1 (and by extension, the rest of the run). If they do not see during Act 1, they have missed their chance to encounter it and it is now impossible to see the event on that run. For a list of all Act Events, see the filtered to a particular Act ( or or ). Shrines Internally, these are still called Shrines . Not to be confused with "events that have Shrine in their name" (such as ), a Shrine is a specific event type all on its own. Shrines include what a player may consider to be a "shrine", but also a few events that "might not seem like shrines". For example, and are shrines, but so is . Shrines can be encountered in any Act, and can only be encountered once per Act. Once the player transitions from one Act to the next, all of the Shrines can be encountered again. For example, if the player encounters in Act 1, it's possible for them to see it again in Act 2 and/or Act 3. Shrines are rarer than Act Events due to how Slay the Spire determines what event to show the player. For more information, see Encountering Events. The is currently not capable of filtering for just this specific type of event; it can only filter for the unofficial Shrine Events type which combines Shrines with One-Time Events. In the meantime, the list of Shrines is provided below for convenience. - - - - - - One-Time Events Internally, these are called Special One Time Events . One-Time Events can only be encountered once per run. They can each have their own Act limitations, although most One-Time Events can be encountered in any Act. For example, is a One-Time Event that can be encountered in any Act. If the player encounters this event in Act 1, they will never be able to encounter it again for the rest of the run even after they transition to Act 2 or Act 3. One-Time Events are rarer than Act Events due to how Slay the Spire determines what event to show the player. For more information, see Encountering Events. This increased rarity is also what separates the One-Time Events which can only be encountered in one specific Act (such as the Act 2-exclusive ) from the normal Act Events. The is currently not capable of filtering for just this specific type of event; it can only filter for the unofficial Shrine Events type which combines Shrines with One-Time Events. In the meantime, the list of One-Time Events is provided below for convenience. Alternatively, use the and disregard any of the Shrines. - - - (only Act 2 and Act 3) - - (only Act 1 and Act 2) - (only Act 2) - - (only Act 2) - - (only Act 3) - - (only Act 2) - - Shrine Events Internally, this is not an event type at all. The Slay the Spire community tends to use the term "Shrine Events" to refer to both Shrines and One-Time Events as if they are a single event type. In reality, Slay the Spire keeps track of Shrines and One-Time Events in separate pools. The term to combine them likely comes from the way that Slay the Spire decides which event to present the player, since when the game determines that a rare event should be encountered it combines the pools for Shrines and the One-Time Events together and then selects a random rare event from that combined pool. For more information, see Encountering Events. For a list of all Shrine Events, see the . Bugs - Shrines are meant to only be encountered at most once per Act. However, there is a bug that allows them to be encountered multiple times per Act. If the player saves and quits the run in the middle of an Act, the next time they continue the run all of the Shrines can be encountered again even if they have already been seen before the save and quit. This is because, unlike the Act Events and One-Time Events, the Shrine pool is not saved and is instead always set as if none of them have been encountered whenever an existing run is loaded. Category:Game Mechanics

title: Keywords
text: Common to all characters Block is a resource used by all characters as well as a number of enemies to reduce damage taken in a turn. Block is removed at the start of your turn unless you are under the effects of or . The Relic also mitigates the end-of-turn loss of Block. Example: Player takes 20 damage while having 8 so the total damage taken will be 20 - 8 or 12. Energy Energy is a resource used to play cards. Every character gains 3 Energy at the start of their turn. Energy can be gained through other Cards or Relics. Every character's cards have their own symbol for Energy. for Ironclad for Silent for Defect for Watcher for all Card Modifiers Exhaust Exhausting a card removes it from play for the rest of combat and places it in the Exhaust pile which can be viewed during combat. Most cards Exhaust themselves on use, however some cards can Exhaust others. The only way to get an Exhausted card back is using the Ironclad card which will bring back one card and then Exhaust itself. Ethereal Ethereal cards are automatically Exhausted if they are in your hand at the end of your turn. Innate Innate is a card modifier that makes that card appear in the first hand of cards you draw during combat. This is not in addition to the default card draw at the start of a turn; an Innate card will take up one of those slots. However, if the number of Innate cards in your deck exceeds the number of cards you draw in your first hand, all the Innate cards will still be drawn, effectively functioning as free card draw. In some cases it may be beneficial to avoid some Innate cards because they can take up the draw slots of potentially more beneficial cards. The , , and Relics have an effect similar to Innate but are different in a few key ways. First of all, when duplicating a bottled card, the other copy will not appear in your hand at the start of combat. Bottled cards cannot be removed from your deck but can be transformed, which will not put the new card in place of the old one. The bottle relic will still say "Start each combat with [name of previous card] in your hand" this is a visual bug and the relic will now have no effect. Unplayable Most as well as a few are unable to be played from your hand. One of the only examples of an unplayable card that is beneficial is , which cannot be played but can be discarded in order to gain Energy. - and are the only relics that allow you to use Unplayble cards. Retain Retain is a card modifier only found on some . Cards with Retain on them will not be discarded at the end of your turn and will persist until played or removed by other means such as . Some will still be discarded at the end of your turn even if Retained. The effects of are similar to Retain, but not strictly speaking the same thing. As such, will not interact with this relic. Character specific Discard While not a defined Keyword in the game, Discard is a mostly Silent exclusive mechanic. Cards can be discarded from your hand into your Discard Pile, in exchange for a buff or to simply cycle them out of your hand. Defect is the only other character with access to Discard, with the card . Discarding your hand at the end of your turn does not trigger Discard mechanics. Watcher's Scry does not trigger Discard mechanics, and only functions as deck cycling. Sources These are the all the sources of Discard in the game: a) Cards , , , , , , , , , , . b) Relics c) Potions Triggers These are everything that triggers on Discard: a) Cards , , , b) Relics , , Orbs Orbs are a mechanic used exclusively by the Defect. Orbs have a passive effect that triggers at the end of your turn. You have a base capacity of 3 Orbs. Channel Channeling an Orb places it in the right-most empty slot. If an Orb is Channeled when all slots are filled, the rightmost Orb will be Evoked to make room. Evoke Consume the right-most Orb to trigger a powerful burst effect. Orb Types - Orbs deal 3 (8 when Evoked) damage to a random enemy. makes Lightning Orbs hit all targets at once. - Orbs give 2 (5 when Evoked) . (Frost Orbs have 3 different sprites depending on which slot they are in, only the center sprite is used here.) - Orbs add 6 damage to its Evoked effect every turn. When Evoked, it will deal that amount of damage to the enemy with the least health. - Orbs give 1 (2 when Evoked) . This Orb is not affected by Focus. Focus is a buff that increases the effect of Orbs (except for ). Focus can be negative. Stance Stances are a mechanic used only by the Watcher. There are 4 Stances (including Neutral/No Stance) each with a significant buff and sometimes a drawback. Some cards and relics change the Watcher to other stances. Stances can be exited using the cards , , or . - Neutral/No Stance: Watcher by default starts with no stance. No bonuses or drawbacks. - : When changing Stances, 2 (3 with ) . - : Deal double damage and take double damage. (Draw 2 Cards ) - : Deal triple damage. Gain 3 . Exit Divinity at the start of your next turn. - Divinity can only be entered by obtaining 10 stacks of , using , or using Scry Scry is a mechanic exclusive to the Watcher. and is defined as this: Look at the top X cards of your draw pile. You may discard any of them. Scrying allows you to see a certain amount of cards into your draw pile, allowing you to see what cards are up next and discard them ahead of time. Cards in the Scry screen will always be shown in draw order, with the leftmost card being what you would draw next. However it will not trigger Discard mechanics, and only functions as deck cycling. Despite the word "discard" being used in the description, Scry does not trigger any discard effects. Category:Game Mechanics

title: Mechanics
text: Resources Health Hit Points (HP): If you run out of HP, you die. The most fundamental resource. HP can take any value from 0 to the player's maximum HP. HP is depleted by taking damage, and may be replenished at Rest Sites, between acts, or with any number of healing effects. Max HP can be raised or lowered by various cards, relics, and events. Increasing max HP will also increase your current HP by the same amount. Read more about Health. Cards Each character comes equipped with a starting deck of 10 or 12 cards. Cards are played during combat. Outside of combat, they may be added, removed, upgraded, or transformed. Some cards or relics can also upgrade cards mid combat. Read more about Cards. Upgrading Upgrading cards makes them more powerful. Cards can only be upgraded once. Read more about Upgrade. Transforming Transformed cards become a random card of any rarity. Read more about Transform rules. Gold Money Pouch: Shows how much gold you're carrying. Gold is the currency within the Spire. Obtained from combat rewards, Events, Chests and certain relics/cards. Can be spent at the Merchant and certain Events to make purchases. Read more about Gold. Potions Use potions during combat to gain bonuses or hinder enemies. Single-use consumables that persist across combats. Read more about Potions. Keys After a run has been won on Ironclad, Silent and Defect, an icon of 3 empty Keys appear to the left of the player's name. To access Act 4, all 3 Keys need to be collected in the same run. They do not have to be collected in the same Act. - 40px Ruby Key : Can be obtained at a Rest Site by clicking the Recall action, instead of Smith or Rest (or , and ) at the same fire. - 30px Emerald Key : Can be obtained by fighting a "super" elite, indicated by a burning animation on a map. Once the Emerald Key is obtained, super elites won't show up in later Acts. - 40px Sapphire Key : Can be obtained at any Chest instead of the Relic in the chest. Will not appear if is active. Will only be linked to the bottom Relic if is active. Trivia: These three keys appear in the game Dead Cells and unlock an outfit resembling the Ironclad. In Combat Card Piles There are three piles in which cards may reside when not in the player's hand. - Draw Pile: The pile from which cards are drawn at the start of the turn. The draw pile is populated at the start of combat with all the cards from the player's deck. Attempting to draw a card when the draw pile is empty triggers a shuffle (see below). - Discard Pile: The pile to which most cards are sent after playing. When a shuffle is triggered, all cards from the discard pile are shuffled into the draw pile. Certain cards do not enter the discard pile after being played: Exhaust cards instead enter the exhaust pile, while cards disappear entirely. - Exhaust Pile: The pile in which cards that have been Exhausted reside. There is no way to recover cards from this pile, except with . Certain enemies have attacks that shuffle cards into the discard or draw piles. Player cards may also shuffle cards into the discard pile (e.g. ) or draw pile (e.g. ). Hand The hand is where cards reside after drawing and before playing. During their turn, players may play cards from the hand, so long as they have the to do so. Hand Size Limit The maximum number of cards allowed in hand is 10. There is no way to exceed this limit. Any draw action that would result in more than 10 cards in the player's hand will result in the remaining cards entering the discard pile immediately. Similarly, anything that creates cards, whether a relic (e.g. ) or another card (e.g. ), will send excess cards to the draw pile, so that the hand size limit is maintained at all times. Turns Combat is turn-based: first the player takes a turn, then the enemy party takes a turn, then repeat. This continues until either the enemy or the player dies. Player Turn At the start of the player's turn, three things happen: # Energy is set to the base energy (default 3), and # A number of cards are drawn (default 5), and # Start-of-turn effects trigger. The default behavior can be changed, typically by Relics (e.g. increases the base energy by 1; increases the number of cards drawn per turn by 2). During their turn, the player may play cards or drink Potions. Cards with an energy cost will subtract that energy from the total. All player actions are resolved immediately upon being performed. When the player presses End Turn, two things happen: # End-of-turn effects trigger. #* This generally resolves in the order relics -> buffs -> cards (e.g. will trigger before , which will trigger before ). # Cards in hand are shuffled into the discard pile. #* This will not occur with or any cards that are , unless they are cards with end-of-turn Effects like or . #** Cards with end-of-turn Effects can sometimes also be at exactly 1 point: during the phase shift. If Awakened One's first phase is killed with an effect that takes place right after the player's turn ends, but before the enemy turn begins (Defect's Lightning Orb passive or ), cards that would normally be discarded like can be . Then the enemy party takes its turn. Enemy Turn Enemy actions during their turn are governed by their Intents, visible as floating symbols above their heads. In multi-enemy encounters, enemies always take actions from left to right. Outside Combat __NOTOC__ Acts Slay the Spire is split up into 3 acts: The Exordium, The City, and The Beyond. Each act is 17 floors long and has certain features in common, such as a Boss encounter at the end. An optional fourth act, The Ending, is unlocked after beating the game once with each of Ironclad, Silent, and Defect (see Keys). Read more about Acts. Floors and Floor Types After completing any mandatory objectives, such as defeating enemies or choosing an option at an Event, the player may proceed to the next floor using the Map interface. Only floors ("nodes") that are connected to the current floor with a dotted line on the map can be accessed. Each floor may be one of seven different types. Read more about Map Locations. Chests Chests are objects which contain some amount of Gold and a Relic. Chests are only obtained through killing bosses, or encountering Treasure Rooms. Boss Chests offer a choice of three different . You can choose to skip this reward. There are three types of Non-Boss chests: Small, Medium and Large. Each has a different chance to give you a Relic and some amount of Gold. Other Ascensions Runs get harder as you win... (Winstreaks are unaffected). See a list of Ascension modifiers here. Category:ToUpdate Category:Game Mechanics

title: Potions
text: Potions are single-use consumables that persist across floors. Most potions provide a boost in a single encounter, and thus can only be used in combat., , and are the only potions that can be used outside of combat. Potions are stored in Potion Slots, of which there are 3 (). The only way to increase the number of Potion Slots is by obtaining . Using a potion does not require and does not count as playing a card. Some potions are exclusive to a single character. Each character has 3 unique potions, with one for each rarity. Potion Rarity In most cases, including playing , the rarity chances for obtaining potions are: , , , and Neow's Second Blessing, as well as and give you a potion uniformly at random, ignoring rarity. Sources Potions may be dropped as a reward after combat. The default chance of a potion dropping is 40% and resets at the start of each Act. If a potion drops, the chance decreases by 10%. If a potion doesn't drop, the chance increases by 10%. - With , a potion will always drop after combat. - When is used or is finished, the game rolls for a potion, but does not display this potion to the player if it drops. Thus, it is impossible to know if the potion chance has increased or decreased, and this uncertainty will persist until the potion chance resets at start of the next act. Cards - Using the card creates 1 random potion (cannot be ). Events - offers you 3 random potions. - allows you to buy 1 random potion for 20 gold, 2 for 30 gold, and 3 for 40 gold. - grants you a random potion at the cost of 10% of your HP (minimum 6). Relics - Obtaining offers you 5 random potions. - Obtaining offers you 1 random potion. Other - fills all your empty potion slots, including its slot, with random potions (cannot generate while in combat). - Potions can be bought at the Merchant. - If you reached the Boss of the first Act in the previous run, Neow may offer you 3 potions. Anti Synergies - Obtaining stops you from obtaining any more potions, including potions obtained from and . However, you are still able to use potions you already have on you. - When a Super Elite is fought with , it never drops a Potion (and the drop chance increases by 10%). Other Interactions - offers you a choice to give Ranwid 1 random potion you have in exchange for a Relic. - heals 5 HP whenever you use a potion. - doubles the effectiveness of potions except: - - - - - - - - cannot be used while you have the Debuff, which is unique to the Spire Shield and Spire Spear fight in Act 4. Update History -Patch V2.0: The Watcher -Ironclad only, Uncommon Potion: -Ironclad only, Rare Potion: -Silent only, Uncommon Potion: -Defect only, Uncommon Potion: -Defect only, Rare Potion: -Watcher only, Common Potion: -Watcher only, Uncommon Potion: -Watcher only, Rare Potion: -Common Potion: -Common Potion: -Uncommon Potion: -Uncommon Potion: -Uncommon Potion: -Rare Potion: -Common potion droprate decreased from 70% -> 65%. -Rare potion droprate increased from 5% -> 10%. - reworked. - healing buffed from 10% -> 20%. - rarity changed from Uncommon -> Common. - healing buffed from 10% -> 30% HP. - rarity changed from Uncommon -> Common. - rarity changed from Uncommon -> Rare. - is now Silent only. -Cards/powers/potions that generate Cards now just describe them with a + in their name. -Several potions now have updated potion bottle assets. -Some potions are rainbow colored now. It's groovy. -Affected potions are , , and . -Uncommon potions now have small circle particle effects. -Rare potions now have gold sparkly visual effects. -Wording updated for several relics, cards, potions, and powers to improve clarity. - can now be used outside of combat. -Fixed filling potions slots with . -Fixed , , , and sometimes not playing cards if original target is dead. -Patch V1.1: The Dealer -While the potion popup is open, the potion's tip is now displayed. - buffed to heal 10% -> 25%. -Potion menu is way bigger now (easier to click on for mouse/touchscreen). -Top panel gold icon, health icon, potion slots, and modifiers now scale up when hovered. -Fixing some grammar and typos in Potion popup text. -Tips for potions now show up when hovered in Reward screens and Shops (not just in the Potion panel after acquisition). -Fixed issue where up/down on controller in potion drink/discard also navigated to monsters/cards during combat. - event can no longer give you a potion if you have the relic. -Weekly Patch 54: Loop -Fixed issue where wouldn't heal the player if used immediately after combat. -Fixed issue where did not give players potions if used post-combat. -Fixed issue where potion seeds weren't persistent on save/load. -Fixed issue where relic wouldn't trigger outside of combat. -Weekly Patch 52: Release Date Price Increase -Fixed issue that allowed player to throw in some rooms to no effect. -Weekly Patch 51: Recursion -Muzzle blight was applying twice to and . -Throwing a at the Final Act boss no longer crashes the game. -Weekly Patch 44: Chrysalis -All sources of random temporary card generation in combat (like ) can no longer generate cards that provide healing. - is now categorized as a healing card. -New tip added to indicate that healing cards cannot be generated randomly during combat. -Weekly Patch 42: Reboot -Adding card, potion, relic, and heart icons that can appear in tips now. Used on Run History screen. -Weekly Patch 41: Chill -Fixed and Colosseum bug that caused the player to go invisible. -Weekly Patch 40: Forethought -Removed two outdated tips about potions. -Weekly Patch 39: We Meet Again! -Potion throw targeting is no longer cancelled via click (you can still drag away or right click to cancel). -Weekly Patch 38: Bane - event no longer allows the player to drink or discard the potion. -You can now drink in more locations. (FRUIT JUICE REVOLUTION) -Weekly Patch 37: Darkness -Neow's Potion option now opens a reward screen with three potions. -Weekly Patch 36: Power Through -Fixed bug where potion removal didn't apply for the left most slot. -Weekly Patch 35: Aggregate -, , and now allow you to choose 1 of 3 cards (buff). -Updated 's use text from Drink -> Throw. -Fixed issue where potion targeting failed if the left most enemy was dead (controller only) - now disables the end turn button so that enemies cannot attack you as you flee. - no longer triggers the Come at Me achievement. -Weekly Patch 34: Malaise - potion buffed. Draw 2 -> 3 cards. -Fixed Save and Quit exploit. -Using a no longer gives the Champion Score bonus. -Weekly Patch 33: Terror - will no longer crash if used with no cards in hand. -Weekly Patch 32: Face Trader -Fixed and not triggering with blight. -Weekly Patch 29: TURBO -Fixed issue where wouldn't heal you if max HP was less than 10. - now work when fighting monsters in ? rooms. -Viewing potions during Discard/Draw Pile view no longer scrolls oddly. -Weekly Patch 28: The Ritual -Ascension 11 modifier changed. Instead of weakening potions, the player starts with 1 less potion slot. -Weekly Patch 27: Hello World -This week adds the long anticipated third character into the game! -New Uncommon Potion: (Defect Only) -Weekly Patch 26: Controller Support Testing - now works properly with as intended. -Weekly Patch 25: Building Blocks - gets an overhaul. It now acts like , but heals. - can now be used outside of combat. - will no longer spawn from . - can no longer spawn . - now spawns potions similarly to now (considers rarity). - now always gives you potions equal to your potion slot count. - can no longer be incorrectly used outside of combat. (Can still be discarded) - wording fix for high ascension levels. - gets an updated color/shape. - shape updated to match . -Weekly Patch 24: Potions?! -16 new potions were added and there are now different rarities for potions! We still have a few more goodies coming for potions in a later patch. -New Daily Mod: Brewmaster -Potion prices are now based on rarity (Common: 50, Uncommon: 75, Rare: 100) -Potions can now be character specific. -Potions now have rarity: Common, Uncommon, and Rare. -New Common Potion: . -New Common Potion: . -New Common Potion: . -New Common Potion: . -New Common Potion: . -New Common Potion: . -New Rare Potion: . -New Rare Potion: . -New Rare Potion: . -New Rare Potion: . -New Rare Potion: . -New Uncommon Potion: (Ironclad Only.) -New Uncommon Potion: . -New Uncommon Potion: . -New Uncommon Potion: (Silent Only.) -New Uncommon Potion: . -Lowered Potion positions by 2px from top. -Updated coloring and image for many potions. -You can now discard potions during combat. -All potions that were in the game before this patch are Common. -Except and , which are now Uncommon. -Weekly Patch 16: Back to Work -Relics and Potions in the shop now correctly set their prices when restocked with . -Weekly Patch 13: Thinking Ahead - now prevents you from acquiring new potions rather than preventing usage (similar to ). -Fixed bug where Nemesis could take more than 1 damage from , Potions, and relics like . -Weekly Patch 11: A Library of Features -If you attempt to get a deprecated potion, you now get a . -Weekly Patch 10: Quality of Life -Adding new . -Removed Elixir Potion from the game. -Weekly Patch 6: We're Back! -Elixir potion buff: Now also affects . -Weekly Patch 5: Happy Holidays! -New Potion, Elixir. -Potions now save using their ID rather than their NAME (wasn't loading in other languages). -You can no longer open the potions dropdown if you're holding a card. -Weekly Patch 4: Full speed ahead! -Added a period at the end of 's description. -Weekly Patch 2: Slimes! - description updated for clarity/consistency. - description updated for clarity/consistency. title: Relics
text: Relics are permanent items that give a passive bonus for the duration of the run. Most relics can be obtained regardless of your character with the exception of: - 11 unique relics for both The Ironclad and The Silent - 9 unique relics for both The Defect and The Watcher. Starter Relics are given to each character when beginning a run. This starter relic can later be replaced with an alternate if you get it as one of your three choices after a Boss. Obtaining - Relics can be obtained from a variety of sources including Elite encounters, chests from random encounters, the Merchant, or using the at Rest Sites. - are only obtainable by killing a Boss, after which you are given a choice of 3 boss relics. - are only obtainable from the Merchant. The third slot in the Merchant's relic offers is always stocked with a . There are 20 shop relics, 4 of which are unique to each character. - are obtained through events (who would've thought) and cannot be obtained by any other means. These are sometimes referred to as Special relics in-game. - include only the which is obtained by collecting or encountering every other relic available (relics can only be encountered once in a run). The is supposed to be obtained when no are left to collect, however it is unobtainable due to a bug in the game's code and can only be obtained with mods. Restrictions - , , and cannot be bought from a shop. - , and will not appear unless you've added an , or to your deck, respectively. - and non-combat Events (including if you get a relic by clicking the option in instead of fighting) can never drop , , and . - Only 2 campfire relics (, , ) can be obtained at once. - 's extra relic can never drop a campfire relic. - 's option can only drop an relic and a relic. - 's extra relic has a 75% chance to be and 25% chance to be . It can never be . - can never restock . 's restocked relics also have pre- prices, regardless of Ascension level. Floor-Based The following relics can only be obtained below certain floors: - Below floor 18 (First floor of Act 2; only obtainable as a Boss Swap or the Act 1 Boss Chest): - Below floor 36 (Floor 2 of Act 3): - Below floor 41 (2 floors below mid-Act Chest in Act 3): , - Below floor 49 (Last Rest Site before Act 3 Boss): , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , - Below floor 53 (Act 4 Shop, or ): Rarity For , , and relics obtained from most sources, the chances are: From Chests, the chances are: Notes - The in-game relic menu only has frame colors for , , and relics. Colors for , and relics were added to this wiki for better clarity, but this is not accurate to how the game presents them. In-game, the , and relics all use the border color instead of the green, pink and indigo colors used here in the wiki. Category:Relics
